Internal Control Officer Job at Hope Payment Service Bank Limited




        
    

        
        Hope Payment Service Bank Limited (Hope PSBank) is Nigeria’s premier Digital-first Bank. Incorporated in September, 2019, Hope PSBank leverages digital technologies to deliver inclusive financial services.

Our belief is that everyone irrespective of education, social or economic status and location should have access to financial and payment services with ease and convenience. We seek to provide the 4As - Accessibility, Affordability, Availability and Awareness of Digital Financial Services.
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    



With Hope PSBank, every mobile telephone user can easily enjoy instant digital financial and payment services with his or her phone number as account number and also a means of payment across all channels – person-to-person on personal devices, web, merchants’ points of sale, automated teller machines and agency outlets.

All transactions on our platforms are gladly settled into accounts of our community of users and customers with any bank of their choice. These are borne out of our philosophy that competition is about service to customers and not about alternative service providers who we view as partners in the service of customers and not as competitors.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Internal Control Officer
Job Location: Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary


	Objectively assessing the company’s business processes
	Ensure accuracy and Integrity of the organization’s records and reports
	Assess the company's risks and the efficacy of its risk management efforts.
	Ensure that the organization is complying with relevant laws.


Responsibilities
Income assurance:


	Operating expense review.
	Transaction monitoring and reviews
	Statutory deduction review- Paye, Pension, and NHF.
	Investment placement review.
	Term Deposit Review
	Fixed Asset reviews
	General ledger review.
	 Proof review
	 Bank Reconciliation Review
	Receipt of goods - invoice verification and vouching.
	Stock/Inventory review
	Maintain and update regulatory rule book
	Regulatory and internal compliance review. 
	Investigation and reporting of Internal Control breaches, fraud and other related compliance issues
	Policy reviews
	Periodic reports to Management


Requirements
Education:


	B.Sc  Degree Accounting, Economics, Finance, Business or any related field
	Desirable: ACA, ACCA, MBA.


Experience:


	2-4 years of related experience.


Skills:
Risk management skills:


	Problem solving skills.
	Accounting experience
	Project management skills.
	Regulatory knowledge.
	Knowledge of MS office suite.
	Budgeting and reporting.
	Communication skills.
	Data analytics.
	Team player.
	Good leadership and people skills.
	Excellent time management.


Method of Application
Interested and qualified candidates should forward their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.

Compliance Officer Job at GTI Investment




        
    

        
        GTI Investment - We are your trusted partner in investment banking services. Our broad range of service offerings comprises Securities Dealing, Asset Management, Trusteeship, Corporate Finance, Private Equity, and Micro banking. Since 1998, we have actively HandHeld our highly diversified client base – Corporations, Financial Institutions, Governments, and Individuals – to a financially secure future.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Compliance Officer

Job Location: Marina, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Description
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	We are looking for an experienced and strategic Compliance Officer to join our fast-growing asset management company.
	The ideal candidate will play a key role in ensuring the company’s operations comply with all applicable laws, regulations, and internal policies.
	This position involves designing, implementing, and overseeing robust compliance programs to protect the company from regulatory risks and maintain its strong reputation in the industry.
	Ensure the firm’s operations comply with all applicable laws, regulations, and guidelines issued by the SEC and other relevant regulatory bodies.


Responsibilities


	Develop and maintain a comprehensive compliance framework to monitor regulatory changes and update internal processes accordingly.
	Conduct internal audits and compliance checks to identify and address potential risks.
	Develop, implement, and regularly review compliance policies and procedures to align with best practices and industry standards.
	Act as the primary liaison with the SEC and other regulatory authorities.
	Oversee the company’s Know Your Customer (KYC) and Anti-Money Laundering (AML) programs.
	Facilitate AML/CFT training for staff
	Investigate compliance breaches and recommend corrective actions.
	Report material compliance issues to the SEC and other regulatory bodies when required.
	Ensure transaction monitoring of staff, employees, and clients’ accounts.
	Work with the business managers to develop an effective, sustainable compliance program.


Requirements


	Bachelor's degree in Finance, Business Administration, Law, or a related field.
	3–6 years of experience in compliance, preferably in an asset management or financial services firm
	The candidate must be an SEC-sponsored individual as a Compliance Officer
	Strong knowledge of local and international financial regulations and compliance requirements.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV and Cover letter to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.

  "description": "Multigate is a financial technology company focused on providing solutions that cut across payments acquisition, processing and remittance as well as treasury and cash management for domestic and international corporates.

1.)&nbsp;Head, Strategic Enterprise Coverage (Manufacturing)

Job Location:&nbsp;Lagos&nbsp;

Description


	Acquire and grow strategic enterprise clients across Sub-Saharan Africa by positioning Multigate&#39;s Treasury &amp; Trade Operating System as the core financial infrastructure powering their treasury, payments, cash &amp; liquidity management, FX, and trade flows.
	This role will secure anchor enterprise clients whose transaction volumes become foundational nodes within the Multigate financial network.
	Success means establishing Multigate as the treasury infrastructure layer for large corporates in the Financial sector.


Key Responsibilities


	Enterprise Origination: Identify, pursue, and close strategic enterprise clients in the Financial sector
	Executive Relationship Development: Develop trusted relationships with:
	CFOs
	Group Treasurers
	Heads of Finance
	Treasury Operations leaders
	Strategic Solution Sales: Lead enterprise discussions positioning Multigate&#39;s infrastructure solutions including:
	Treasury (Cash &amp; Liquidity Management)
	Auto-Reconciled Receivables Collection
	Multi-Bank Connectivity
	Supply Chain Finance
	Trade Finance Management
	FX &amp; Cross-Border Payment Infrastructure
	Complex Enterprise Deal Structuring: Lead negotiations and commercial structuring for:
	Multi-year enterprise infrastructure agreements
	Platform licensing agreements
	Strategic network onboarding initiatives


Key Performance Metrics
Performance will be evaluated quarterly using the following metrics:


	Annual Recurring Revenue Closed
	Anchor Enterprise Clients
	Average Contract Value
	Enterprise ERP Payment Flows Onboarded
	Vendor Network Onboarding


Requirements
Required Experience:


	Minimum 10 years of experience in enterprise financial infrastructure sales or corporate banking solutions
	Proven track record of closing deals exceeding $250K+ annual contract value
	Typical backgrounds include:
	Transaction Banking Sales
	Treasury Solutions Sales
	Trade Finance Sales
	Corporate Banking Product Sales


Competencies (Must-Have Skills &amp; Behaviors):


	Enterprise Solution Selling: Ability to lead consultative infrastructure sales involving:
	Complex treasury workflows
	Multi-bank environments
	Regulatory constraints
	Strategic Financial Acumen: Deep understanding of:
	Treasury management
	Liquidity management
	Cross-border payments
	FX markets
	Trade finance structures
	Executive Negotiation: Demonstrated ability to negotiate with C-suite executives and influence large enterprise decisions
	Network Development: Ability to open doors with corporate treasurers, CFOs, and finance leaders across strategic sectors
	Structured Pipeline Management: Disciplined approach to:
	Forecasting
	Opportunity management
	Revenue planning
	Commercial Storytelling: Ability to articulate clear economic value propositions to enterprise clients


Cultural Alignment (Multigate Values):
Candidates must demonstrate strong alignment with Multigate&#39;s core values:


	Customer Obsession: Prioritizes long-term client trust and value creation
	Excellence in Action: Maintains extremely performance standards
	Agility &amp; Innovation: Adapts quickly to regulatory and financial market dynamics
	Trust &amp; Accountability: Operates with transparency and ownership
	Empowerment &amp; Ownership: takes charge of sales outcomes and team results.


Key Outcomes (What Success looks like)
The following outcomes define success in this role.


	Revenue Origination:
	Secure $6M+ Annual Recurring Revenue from enterprise clients.
	Target average contract value (ACV): $300K-$1M per client.
	Anchor Enterprise Acquisition:


Acquire 5-8 anchor enterprise clients per year. These clients should typically have:


	Annual payment flows &gt; $500M
	Multi-bank treasury complexity
	Cross-border trade flows
	Network Transaction Flows
	Drive onboarding of enterprise clients that collectively route $5B+ annual payment and FX flows through the Multigate platform.
	Platform Penetration:


Each enterprise client should adopt 2-4 Multigate infrastructure modules, such as:


	Treasury (Cash &amp; Liquidity Management)
	Auto-Reconciled Receivables Collection
	Multi-Bank Connectivity
	Supply Chain Finance
	Trade Finance Management
	FX &amp; Cross-Border Payment Infrastructure
	Strategic Pipeline Discipline
	Maintain 3-5x pipeline coverage of target revenue.
	Develop relationships with 20+ enterprise CFOs / Treasurers annually.
	Vendor Network Expansion (via our Supply Chain Finance [Digital Invoice Discounting Solution])
	Enable enterprise clients to onboard supplier ecosystems onto Multigate infrastructure.
	Target vendor network onboarding: 1,000+ vendors per enterprise client where applicable.
